publishedAbstract: OpenShift Data Foundation has two Disaster Recovery solutions that use replication to protect and recover stateful applications. Metropolitan Disaster Recovery uses a Red Hat Ceph Storage stretched cluster to perform synchronous data replication. Regional Disaster Recovery performs asynchronous replication between two independent Red Hat Ceph Storage clusters. The [OpenShift Data Foundation documentation](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_openshift_data_foundation/4.15/html/configuring_openshift_data_foundation_disaster_recovery_for_openshift_workloads/index) provides more information about each of these products. When [OpenShift Virtualization](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.15/virt/about_virt/about-virt.html) Virtual Machines are part of a stateful application they can also be protected by OpenShift Data Foundation Disaster Recovery with some limitations. For best results follow the best practices that are outlined in this article.
publishedTitle: Use OpenShift Data Foundation Disaster Recovery to Protect Virtual Machines
